info.title: Vantage Server Protocol
info.summary: Read-only world-rendering data plane for Minecraft launchers and server hosts.
info.description:
Protocol v1 exposes discovery, health, world metadata, and immutable render artifacts. A Vantage process currently serves one world with the id `default`. Player identity and authorization remain the responsibility of an upstream authenticating host.

Every endpoint (including the public discovery ones) additionally enforces a request policy that can answer before the documented responses: `403` when a browser `Origin` header is present but not on the configured allowlist, `400` when a security-relevant header (`Origin`, `Authorization`, `Access-Control-Request-Method`) appears more than once, `405` for methods other than GET/HEAD/OPTIONS, and `414` for request targets over 1024 bytes.

get.summary: Discover the protocol and authentication mode

get.description: A successful response means the HTTP process is alive. It does not grant world access or guarantee that a particular tile can be rendered.

get.summary: List worlds exposed to this connection

get.description: Continuous manifests set `dynamic` and `rendering` to true and attach an opaque `revision` string to each tile. Clients poll the manifest and replace only coordinates whose revision changed. Every response carries a strong `ETag` over the exact body; polling with `If-None-Match` answers `304` while the catalog is unchanged. `ETag` is exposed to cross-origin scripts via `Access-Control-Expose-Headers`.

parameters.name: If-None-Match
parameters.description: Entity-tag list from a previous manifest response. A match short-circuits to `304 Not Modified` with no body.

ETag.description: Strong validator over the exact manifest body.

304.description: The manifest is byte-identical to the presented validator; no body.

head.summary: Probe the manifest path without producing it
head.description: HEAD never reaches the dynamic producer, and the live manifest is never persisted to the cache directory, so this always answers 404. Use GET to read the manifest.

get.summary: Read the current shared texture array

parameters.name: tile
parameters.description: Tile filename in the form `t.<signed-x>.<signed-z>.vtile`, with coordinates in canonical decimal form (no leading zeros, no `+`, no `-0`). Only coordinates advertised by the manifest can start a bake.

get.summary: Read or render one advertised geometry tile
get.description: A cache hit is returned directly. A miss is rendered under Vantage's memory and concurrency admission limits. Concurrent requests for the same tile share one in-flight bake. Responses for manifest-advertised tiles carry a strong `ETag` combining the tile's manifest `revision` with an opaque per-process session tag, and are `private, no-cache` so a client may keep a copy it must revalidate. A syntactically valid coordinate the manifest does not advertise is answered with an empty `200` carrying no `ETag` and the default `private, no-store`, since there is no revision to validate against. Presenting that validator answers `304` without transferring, reading, or — on a tile that has not been baked yet — rendering anything. The session tag is part of the validator because tile payloads index the texture array built by the process that baked them: after a restart the same world revision is deliberately not revalidatable.

parameters.name: If-None-Match
parameters.description: Entity tag from a previous response for this tile. A match short-circuits to `304 Not Modified` with no body and no bake.

200.description: Gzip-wrapped Vantage tile. A zero-byte response represents an advertised tile that meshed to empty terrain.

Cache-Control.description: `private, no-cache` for a manifest-advertised tile, so a client may store a copy it must revalidate. A well-formed coordinate the manifest does not advertise keeps the default `private, no-store`.

ETag.description: Strong validator over this tile's `{session, revision}` pair. Absent for a coordinate the manifest does not advertise, which has no revision to validate against.

304.description: The client already holds this tile revision from this session; no body.

head.summary: Inspect an existing cache entry without starting a bake

200.description: The tile cache entry exists; Content-Length is derived from file metadata

bearerAuth.description: Direct deployments send the server-specific secret in `Authorization: Bearer`. In proxy mode the trusted host authenticates the player and protects the private Vantage hop.

WorldId.name: worldId
WorldId.description: Protocol v1 currently exposes one world named `default`.

NotFound.description: World or cache entry not found

WorldManifest.description: The Vantage manifest format is shared with static renders. Server manifests add continuous-render fields and tile revisions.

revision.description: Opaque source revision; compare for equality only.
